title: Heading elements should include accessible content
description: Heading elements are represented by the tags <h1> through <h6>, with <h1> being the highest level and <h6> being the lowest. These elements are used to structure the content of the page and create a hierarchical outline that can be followed by users and search engines alike. In the context of accessibility, heading elements play a crucial role.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.
impact: Accessibility barrier for screen readers and users with disabilities

checks.message: Heading elements should include accessible content for screen readers and SEO
Empty heading element - add descriptive text content for accessibility
Heading contains only whitespace - add meaningful text content
Heading element set via innerHTML/outerHTML is empty - add accessible text content
Setting heading text content to empty string - provide meaningful text for accessibility
